member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.Common.Classification.ReqnrollClassificationTypeNames
member.summary: The canonical set of custom Reqnroll classification / semantic-token type names.

remarks.para: These names are the single source of truth shared by:

list.item: the LSP server's semantic-token legend (advertised in the initialize response), and
the Visual Studio extension's MEF ClassificationTypeDefinition exports (DeveroomClassifications), which map each name to a concrete editor colour.

remarks.para: Rather than emitting the generic LSP standard token types (keyword, string, parameter, …), the server declares these Reqnroll-specific names so that each IDE client can map a Reqnroll concept to a Reqnroll-specific colour instead of overloading a host theme's generic scopes. The values intentionally match the custom classification names already used by the existing Reqnroll.VisualStudio extension, giving existing users pixel-for-pixel continuity.

Legend stability: the order in is the contract between the server and every client (the index emitted in each semantic-token 5-tuple is an index into this list). Append new entries only — never reorder or remove existing ones — so that older client index assumptions remain valid across versions.

member.summary: Gets or sets the step parameter.

member.summary: Gets or sets the scenario outline placeholder.

member.name: F:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.Common.Classification.ReqnrollClassificationTypeNames.UndefinedStep
member.summary: Step text of a step with no matching binding. Only carries meaning once Roslyn/C# source-level binding discovery is available; reserved in the legend from Phase 1 so the legend never changes across phases.

member.name: F:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.Common.Classification.ReqnrollClassificationTypeNames.AmbiguousStep
member.summary: Step text of a step that matches more than one step-definition binding (ambiguous). The test would fail at runtime with "Ambiguous step definitions"; highlighted to make the conflict visible in the editor.

member.name: F:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.Common.Classification.ReqnrollClassificationTypeNames.Ordered
member.summary: The legend order. Indices into this array are the tokenTypeIndex emitted in the LSP semantic-token 5-tuples. Append-only (see "Legend stability" in the type remarks).

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.Common.Logging.ITelemetryDebugLog
member.summary:
Debug-only sink that mirrors telemetry events to a local file for later review.
Deliberately independent of the analytics opt-out gate (REQNROLL_TELEMETRY_ENABLED): it records what the system produced even when transmission is disabled or the event is dropped downstream. Implementations must never throw — telemetry debugging must not break the feature path.

member.name: P:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.Common.Logging.ITelemetryDebugLog.IsEnabled
member.summary: Whether a sink is configured. When false, is a no-op.

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.Common.Logging.ITelemetryDebugLog.Record(System.String,System.String,System.Object,System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.String)
member.summary: Appends one record describing a telemetry event.
member.param: Where the event was captured: "server" or "host".
The event properties (serialized as a JSON object); may be null.
Host only: whether the opt-out gate allowed transmission.
Host only: whether the event was actually handed to the channel.
Host only: the message if transmission threw, otherwise null.

member.name: P:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.Common.ProjectSystem.NuGetVersion.RequestedRange
member.summary: The project's requested package range for the package.
member.remarks: If the project uses packages.config, this will be same as the installed package version. If the project uses PackageReference, this is the version string in the project file, which may not match the resolved package version, and may not be single version string. If the project uses PackageReference, and the package is a transitive dependency, the value will be null.

member.name: T:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.Common.ProjectSystem.PathUtils
member.summary: PathUtils

member.name: M:Reqnroll.IdeSupport.Common.ProjectSystem.PathUtils.IsUnderFolder(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: True when is itself, or lives somewhere under it — with a directory-separator boundary check, unlike a bare filePath.StartsWith(folder).
member.remarks: A plain string-prefix check treats a sibling folder whose name happens to extend the prefix as "inside" it — e.g. @"C:\Repo\Minimalnet481\Foo.cs" starts with @"C:\Repo\Minimal" even though Minimalnet481 is a completely different folder than Minimal. Confirmed live: this let a sibling project's step-definition bindings bleed into another project's registry, producing false "ambiguous step" matches.
